strength.title: Strength assessment
strength.subtitle: Five-lift assessment with per-lift scores and a combined radar-axis score.
strength.repsAccuracyNudge: Fewer reps usually make the estimated 1RM more reliable. If you have a heavier, lower-rep set taken near failure, prefer entering that weight and rep count.
strength.fieldsHint: Uses gender, age, and body weight from your Home physical profile. Leave both fields empty for a lift you are skipping. Reps above 10 are not accepted (submaximal estimate quality). Each lift also has a model weight ceiling — values above it are clamped for scoring and storage, with an on-screen notice.
strength.combinedHint: Averages every row that has both weight and reps. Rows with one field empty are ignored here (but must not be “half filled” — clear both or fill both).
strength.capWeightNotice: {{lift}}: you entered {{input}} kg, which exceeds this movement’s model ceiling ({{max}} kg). Scoring and saved weight use {{max}} kg — aligned with elite / all-time raw or strongman reference context for this lift (not a meet certification).
strength.branchLine: {{weight}} kg × {{reps}} reps · est. 1RM {{oneRm}} kg · lift score {{score}}
strength.nextMilestoneHint: Next Milestone: {{points}} pts until next tier upgrade.
strength.radarClampNote: If the mean exceeds the per-axis cap (0–200), the stored radar value is clamped.
strength.submitDone: Saved — Home radar and merged scores use this strength axis.
strength.submitDoneWithScore: Saved (strength axis {{score}}) — Home radar and merged scores are updated.
strength.profileIncompleteHint: Strength scoring needs a complete Home profile (sex, age, height, weight).

howToInfo.title: Scoring notes
howToInfo.intro: Each completed lift is converted to an estimated 1RM (Brzycki), then to a DOTS-style score adjusted for age (McCulloch) and normalized against built-in anchor totals for that lift — matching the reference fitness stack.
howToInfo.reps: Use 1–10 reps so the 1RM estimate stays in a reliable range; enter the weight you actually lifted for that set.
howToInfo.repsAccuracy: Within that range, lower rep counts are generally more trustworthy for Brzycki extrapolation; very high-rep sets can yield a more optimistic estimated 1RM, which may not match a “heavier is always stronger” gut check.
howToInfo.tip: Warm up and use consistent depth / range of motion when comparing over time. This screen is not a competition verification tool.

errors.missing-profile: Add a complete physical profile on Home before calculating strength.
errors.no-inputs: Enter at least one lift with both weight and reps.
errors.pair-incomplete: For each lift, enter both weight and reps, or leave both fields empty.
errors.invalid-weight: Enter a positive weight (kg) for each completed lift.
errors.invalid-reps: Enter a valid rep count for each completed lift.
errors.reps-out-of-range: Reps must be between 1 and 10 for each completed lift.

singleErrors.missing-profile: Add a complete physical profile on Home first.
singleErrors.empty-row: Enter weight and reps for this lift, then calculate.
singleErrors.pair-incomplete: Enter both weight and reps for this lift, or clear both fields.
singleErrors.invalid-weight: Enter a positive weight (kg) for this lift.
singleErrors.invalid-reps: Enter a valid rep count for this lift.
singleErrors.reps-out-of-range: Reps must be between 1 and 10 for this lift.
